source_rationale: Recently, single cell genomics, including directly in human embryos, has reinforced the idea that progenitors contributing to the FHF and SHF are actually made up of subpopulations with discrete identities and fate assignments,8–10 such as the juxta-cardiac field (JCF) which overlaps the FHF and is marked by the basic helix-loop-helix (bHLH) TF, Hand1.

To deconvolute this developmental diversity, we created a dual cardiac-endoderm fluorescent reporter hESC line by taking an NKX2-5-GFP reporter hESC line and introducing a knock-in SOX17-T2A-dTomato (Figure S1A).

In this triple reporter, FOXA2-BFP was entirely co-expressed with SOX17-Tom at day 7.5 when NKX2-5-GFP+ cells emerged (Figure S1C), consistent with data in early human embryos,10 and when isolated at day 5, SOX17+ FOXA2+ cells generated very few cardiomyocytes suggesting these were largely not cardiac progenitors (Figures S1D and S1E).
Recognition motifs of GATA, CTCF, MEIS/PBX, MEF2, NKX2-5, and TBX20 were significantly enriched in the cardiac GFP+ population.
Recognition motifs of GATA, CTCF, MEIS/PBX, MEF2, NKX2-5, and TBX20 were significantly enriched in the cardiac GFP+ population.
In contrast, motifs of TEAD, WT1, HOX, and HAND were enriched in the GFP-neg Tom-neg population.
We attribute the HAND motif enrichment to the expression of HAND1, and the HOX motif enrichment primarily to the expression of the HOXB cluster, principally HOXB1–6 (Figures S2B–D).
The SOX17+ population was dominated by the enrichment of motifs and associated co-expression of endoderm TFs FOXA2, SOX17, and HNF4A (Figures 2A, S2B, and S2E).
The SOX17+ population was dominated by the enrichment of motifs and associated co-expression of endoderm TFs FOXA2, SOX17, and HNF4A (Figures 2A, S2B, and S2E).
The SOX17+ population was dominated by the enrichment of motifs and associated co-expression of endoderm TFs FOXA2, SOX17, and HNF4A (Figures 2A, S2B, and S2E).
We noted HAND1, TBX2 and HCN4 expression on the FHF-like lineage (Figures 4B).

The SHF-like lineage showed prominent ISL1, FGF10, FOXC1/2 and JAG1 expression (Figure 4B), which are well-established SHF markers.

The SHF-like lineage showed prominent ISL1, FGF10, FOXC1/2 and JAG1 expression (Figure 4B), which are well-established SHF markers.

Whereas the SHF-like lineage appeared to progress solely to cardiomyocytes, the FHF-like lineage – which at its early stage included a strong JCF-like signature (HAND1, MAB21L2, BNC2 and MSX1/2)8,11,12 – displayed multilineage fate also giving rise to epicardial and fibroblast-like cells.

Additionally, we detected a side branch from the FHF/JCF-like progenitors with high transient CDX2, GATA2, and TBX3 expression and low TBX5, which may represent an extraembryonic mesoderm-like lineage (Figures 4A' and 4B).

The SHF-like lineage was characterized by increased activity of FOXC1/2, SIX1, PRRX2 and MEIS1 regulons, and regulons controlled by regulators of cell proliferation including E2F family members (Figure 4F and 4G).

At these concentrations we noted that WT1+ mesothelial / epicardial cells developed in control conditions and following the addition of SB, but not following DMH1 treatment, whereas cardiomyocytes formed in all conditions (Figure 1H).

In contrast, the early FHF/JCF-like lineage was characterized by increased activity of HOX, JDP2, MSX1/2, FOXF1 and LEF1 regulons and many other regulons which were later enriched in the epicardial or fibroblast-like cells such as CREB3L1 (Figure 4F and 4G).

Comparing the cardiomyocytes in the wild-type to the HAND1-null revealed that the dominant regulons were controlled largely by the same factors; this included classical cardiac TFs MEF2, SRF, GATA4, and TBX5; and metabolic regulators including estrogen-related receptors (ESRRA and ESRRG) and the transcriptional co-activator PGC-1α (PPARGC1A).

However, whereas GFP+ cells in these conditions had a myocardial-commitment signature of high SRF, MEF2C, and MYOCD expression, the GFP-neg cells had higher HAND1 and other markers described to demarcate the JCF, proepicardium, septum transversum mesenchyme, and extraembryonic mesoderm11,12.

In contrast, with DMH1, emerging GFP+ Tom-neg cells displayed an anterior SHF-like profile with high FGF8 and FGF10 expression and an absence of posterior HOXB1.
In contrast, with DMH1, emerging GFP+ Tom-neg cells displayed an anterior SHF-like profile with high FGF8 and FGF10 expression and an absence of posterior HOXB1.

A small number of WT1+ (EPI) and DCN+ (FLC) cells clustered together, possibly explained by partial compensation from HAND2 which was ectopically activated in 10% of the HAND1-null mesoderm, but the development of FHF/JCF-like and extraembryonic-like progenitors and epicardial and fibroblast-like cell derivatives, was almost entirely lost.

A small number of WT1+ (EPI) and DCN+ (FLC) cells clustered together, possibly explained by partial compensation from HAND2 which was ectopically activated in 10% of the HAND1-null mesoderm, but the development of FHF/JCF-like and extraembryonic-like progenitors and epicardial and fibroblast-like cell derivatives, was almost entirely lost.

The main lineage of progenitors in the HAND1-null had a SHF-like signature with an equivalent or higher frequency of FGF10+ (51% vs 46%) ISL1+ (43% vs 43%), and TBX1+ (18% vs 10%) cells compared to the wild-type SHF-like lineage (Figures 4A' and 4C').

TBX2 lineage tracing showed it marks cells which make a similar contribution to the left ventricle as HAND1,28 and HCN4 is recognized as an FHF progenitor marker.
